task_completion.description: The agent opened NREL's System Advisor Model (SAM) application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SAM graphical user interface is visible on the screen, typically showing the welcome screen or a new project setup.

task_completion.description: The agent selected Phoenix weather data for the simulation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the SAM 'Location and Data' tab, 'Phoenix' (or a Phoenix, AZ weather file) is selected and visible in the location settings.

task_completion.description: The agent configured the DC system size to approximately 5 kW.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the SAM 'System Design' tab, the Nameplate DC capacity or desired array size is set to a value between 4.5 and 5.5 kW.

task_completion.description: The agent set the roof slope (tilt) to ~20 degrees and orientation (azimuth) to south-facing (~180 degrees).
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the SAM 'System Design' tab, the tilt/slope field shows a value between 15 and 25 degrees, and the azimuth field shows a value between 175 and 185 degrees.

task_completion.description: The agent successfully ran the simulation to generate the annual energy production.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SAM 'Results' page is visible, displaying an annual energy production metric (expected to be between 7000 and 9500 kWh).

task_completion.description: The agent saved the configuration and simulation results to the specified JSON file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal, file explorer, or text editor shows the file '/home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/Phoenix_Residential_5kW.json' being created or edited, containing the location, system parameters, and annual energy production (kWh).

integrity.description: The agent used the SAM GUI to perform the simulation rather than just writing a mock JSON file directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the agent interacting with the SAM graphical interface (setting parameters, clicking simulate) prior to creating the JSON file.

integrity.description: The annual energy production in the JSON file matches the actual results generated by the SAM GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The kWh value written in the final JSON file matches the annual energy production value displayed in the SAM Results tab.

integrity.description: The agent did not hallucinate or hardcode generic data into the JSON file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The JSON file contains specific values derived from the actual SAM simulation run rather than placeholder text or perfectly rounded guesses.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file is /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/Phoenix_Residential_5kW.json. The location must be Phoenix. The DC system size should be between 4.5 and 5.5 kW. The tilt should be between 15 and 25 degrees. The azimuth should be between 175 and 185 degrees (south-facing). The simulated annual energy production should be between 7000 and 9500 kWh (empirically around 8700 kWh).
